target.name: StoreDot
target.founder: Doron Myersdorf, Simon Litsyn, Gil Rosenman

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $206M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
StoreDot is a Series E climate and energy company that has raised $206.5M to date. The broader sector comprises 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, a narrowly-filtered peer set yielding three or more same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables could not be constructed, limiting reliance on multiples-based approaches.

The stage-progression method is applicable here. Using total capital raised ($206M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E to subsequent-stage transitions yields $289M. This method assumes that capital deployed correlates with value creation and that StoreDot's progression trajectory aligns with sector norms. The peer-median method was deemed not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow peer universe; drawing on all-sector medians would introduce undue noise.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $246M (low) to $332M (high), with a median of $289M. This range reflects a ±15% confidence band around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for StoreDot's specific technology maturity and market conditions, the relevance of the broader peer set given heterogeneity in climate-tech business models, and whether capital raised adequately proxies enterprise value absent revenue or path-to-profitability visibility.
